name: Hong Kong Housing Crisis
description: A socioeconomic simulation of Hong Kong's housing affordability crisis. Stakeholders include property developers, government officials, NGOs, and young renters. Explores policy intervention, public sentiment, and market dynamics.
seed_text: Hong Kong faces a severe housing affordability crisis. Property prices rank among the highest globally, with average households spending over 70% of income on rent. The government has proposed new public housing policies while property developers lobby against restrictions. NGOs advocate for tenants' rights while young professionals consider emigration.
